description: The Trump administration has agreed to award $1 billion to International Business Machines Corp. to build a foundry for producing quantum computing chips, part of a broad strategy to bolster US leadership in an emerging industry.

title: Swiggy shareholders push back against more power to founder CEO Sriharsha Majety
description: Swiggy shareholders have rejected a proposal that would’ve given founder CEO Sriharsha Majety disproportionate control of the board, even if his stake fell to 2.45%.

title: Early investors look to sell Paytm shares worth $110 million via block deal
description: The stake sale marks another gradual exit by early backers as investor sentiment improves after Paytm returned to profitability following regulatory setbacks.

title: LIC sees West Asia war hitting insurance growth amid lower savings
description: LIC expects the West Asia crisis to weigh on renewal premium growth.

title: CESL to supply 300 electric water tankers to Delhi Jal Board
description: The initiative is aligned with Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s call for the adoption of electric vehicles, says CESL.

title: Maruti Suzuki to hike prices across models by up to  ₹30,000 from June 2026
description: Maruti Suzuki will increase prices of its models across its portfolio by up to  ₹30,000 with effect from June 2026.

title: KG Basin row: RIL claims returns to govt surpass what ONGC could have delivered
description: The RIL-led consortium defended its extraction of ‘fugitive’ gas as a natural occurrence, arguing that its timely development prevented a $1.4 billion loss to the state and provided superior profit-sharing compared to a potential ONGC operation.

title: ITC revenue grows 17% as cigarette tax bites, consumer brands surge
description: While the cigarettes division posted healthy numbers, the firm cautioned that high taxes imposed in Feb may encourage illicit sales. Meanwhile, ITC’s packaged consumer brands business grew rapidly in the March 2026 quarter. However, the Iran war may dampen growth across verticals

title: United Nations top court says right to strike is protected by a key labor treaty
description: United Nations' top court says right to strike is protected by a key labor treaty

title: Mamaearth parent proposes  ₹3-per-share dividend, its first ever, after record profit in Q4
description: The company will return nearly  ₹98 crore to shareholders as net profit surged 178% in the March quarter, driven by a record performance from younger brands like The Derma Co.

title: QSR chains brace for slower growth, margin pressure after weak FY26 demand
description: QSR chains are shifting focus from rapid expansion to profitability, emphasizing value meals and cost management in response to changing consumer spending habits.

title: Amazon India targets beauty boom with 100 global brands as online rivalry rises
description: &nbsp;The premium beauty segment is growing rapidly, with significant demand from tier-two and tier-three cities, and rising consumer spending projected to make India's beauty market worth $40 billion by 2030.

title: Odisha NBFC Annapurna Finance to raise $75-100 mn to facilitate exits for early backers
description: The microlender's last equity fundraise was more than two years ago when Piramal Alternatives Trust, a wholly owned subsidiary of Piramal Enterprises, acquired a 10.4% stake in Annapurna for  ₹300 crore.

title: R K Swamy bets on integrated advertising model as global agency giants split media and creative
description: In a tough financial year where big advertising networks underwent layoffs and AI-led disruption, R K Swamy posted a 15% jump in annual revenue in FY26 and a 30% jump in profits excluding tax and exceptional items.&nbsp;

title: SpiceJet faces fresh legal heat as engine lessor seeks enforcement of $8 million UK award
description: Sunbird France moved the Delhi High Court to enforce a London court ruling over unpaid engine lease rentals and maintenance dues, adding to SpiceJet’s mounting legal and financial troubles.

title: Elon Musk hiring engineers for SpaceXAI, says no prior AI experience needed: ‘Smart humans figure it out fast’
description: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk has launched a hiring push for “world-class engineers/physicists” for SpaceXAI, saying applicants do not need prior AI experience. The move signals SpaceX’s growing focus on artificial intelligence across rockets, Starlink and future autonomous systems.

title: AMD invests $10 Billion in Taiwan to build AI chips and challenge Nvidia's dominance
description: Advanced Micro Devices has pledged more than $10 billion in Taiwan to expand AI chip production with partners including TSMC and ASE Technology, as the chipmaker intensifies its challenge to Nvidia's dominance in artificial intelligence computing.

title: FMCG products set to cost more if global pressures persist, hurting consumption
description: Experts said Indian FMCG products may become costlier due to reduced raw material stocks and rising inflation linked to the Iran-US conflict. Significant price hikes in dairy and staples reflect broader inflationary pressures, while household consumption growth may slow amid economic uncertainty.

title: Scapia raises $63 million led by General Catalyst to scale AI-led travel fintech play
description: Travel fintech startup Scapia has raised $63 million in a Series C round led by General Catalyst to scale AI-led products, expand partnerships and grow its Gen Z user base.

title: K Hospitality launches investment arm with  ₹200 cr corpus to back consumer companies
description: K Hospitality Corp has launched Kliff Ventures with a  ₹200 crore corpus to invest in early-stage consumer and retail brands across food, lifestyle and allied sectors.

title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan Chase will hire more AI specialists, fewer traditional bankers
description: Jamie Dimon said JPMorgan will increasingly hire AI specialists and fewer traditional bankers as artificial intelligence reshapes the financial sector. The bank is expanding investments in AI across operations, joining a wider Wall Street trend of prioritising tech talent and automation.

title: From Saudi Aramco to Alibaba: 7 biggest IPOs before Elon Musk’s SpaceX market debut
description: SpaceX could become the biggest IPO in history, but before Elon Musk’s space giant hits public markets, companies like Saudi Aramco, Alibaba, Visa and Meta dominated global listings. Here’s a look at the seven largest IPOs ever by funds raised.

title: Mark Zuckerberg 'feeling weight of' mass layoffs at Meta, says ‘success isn’t a given’ in memo
description: Mark Zuckerberg sent a formal email to his staff ahead of the layoffs, addressing it and thanking those who have been sacked for their work.

title: Marriott bets on outbound Indian travel surge, rewires loyalty into experiences
description: Marriott International is focusing on the Indian market as travel demand grows, especially among younger travellers. The company is transforming its loyalty programs to prioritize experiential rewards and has formed partnerships with various sectors to enhance customer engagement.

title: Microfinance sector recovers after stress cycle but caution prevails amid geopolitical, inflation risks
description: India's microfinance institutions are cautiously recovering after two years of stress, with improved collection efficiency and asset quality. Analysts warn that while conditions are stabilizing, growth will be disciplined due to geopolitical risks and a changing lending environment.

title: Tata Capital listing cushions Tata Sons in a difficult FY26
description: The one-off boost from Tata Capital's listing comes even as Tata Trusts Chairman Noel Tata raises concern over mounting losses in Tata Digital to Air India.

title: Elon Musk's SpaceX files for what could be biggest IPO in stock market history - Key takeaways
description: SpaceX, the rocket and satellite company, discloses finances for the first time, revealing $18.7 billion in revenue, $4.9 billion in losses, and ambitions to target a $28.5 trillion addressable market.
